summary: Soaring Estimated Prophet and epic Other One → Morning Dew with mixed first set
blurb: The second set shines with a soaring Estimated Prophet and an electrifying The Other One → Morning Dew. Jerry delivers powerful leads on the jams but slips on Terrapin Station and Crazy Fingers in the first set. Overall the show is solid despite uneven moments.
review:
On March 26, 1995 at The Omni, the Dead delivered a night of highs and lows that still holds interest for fans. The first set rolled through familiar terrain—Cold Rain And Snow, Little Red Rooster, Ramble On Rose—and gave us a sweet Lazy River Road that many reviewers praised for its melodic lift. Unfortunately, the set was marred by a sloppy Terrapin Station and a tentative Crazy Fingers, where Jerry’s guitar seemed to miss verses and lose some of his usual dexterity.

The real magic erupted in the second set. A tight Samson & Delilah opened the way into an expansive jam that built through It’s All Too Much into a blistering Estimated Prophet, which reviewers called “soaring.” From there the band launched into a thunderous The Other One → Morning Dew sequence that was described as “electrifying” and “unforgettable,” with Jerry’s vocal intensity and guitar work soaring over a psychedelic drums‑>space bridge. The night closed on a high‑energy Johnny B. Goode, leaving the audience buzzing despite the occasional rough spots earlier in the show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings were clearly positive (highlighting Estimated Prophet, Lazy River Road, Other One > Morning Dew) while one was mixed. The predominance of praise for the second‑set jams and overall solid performance yields a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with clear mix, praised for sweet clarity and the standout Other One → Morning Dew sequence.

key_highlights: Estimated Prophet – a soaring, high‑energy performance that lifts the second set.
The Other One → Morning Dew – an electrifying, psychedelic jam highlighted by a tight drums→space transition.
Lazy River Road – melodic and memorable, cited as a highlight of the first set.
Johnny B. Goode – a rousing encore that ends the night on a high note.

band_performance.Jerry: Powerful on Estimated Prophet and The Other One, delivering soaring leads and intense vocals on Morning Dew; however his playing slips on Terrapin Station and Crazy Fingers, missing verses and showing reduced dexterity.
band_performance.Vince: Vocals are a bit forward in the mix, which some listeners found slightly over‑present, but otherwise no major keyboard issues noted.
band_performance.Mickey: Alongside Bill, provides a tight, psychedelic drums→space bridge that fuels the second‑set momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers solid drumming throughout; together with Mickey creates the standout rhythmic flow into The Other One.
